skill.name: dependency-upgrade
skill.description: Plan and execute dependency upgrades with compatibility checks, staged changes, rollback planning, and test coverage guidance.
skill.summary: Guide dependency upgrades with compatibility analysis, staged rollout steps, and validation planning.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell commands, one network URL, file operations, and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are instructional examples for dependency upgrades, with weak cryptography alerts caused by ordinary words such as description or describe. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because copied commands can install packages, rewrite files, fetch remote changelogs, or remove local branches.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Commands Modify Dependencies
medium_findings.description: The skill shows npm, yarn, npx, and install commands that can modify dependency manifests, lock files, or installed packages. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should review commands before execution and avoid untrusted package names.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in documentation blocks and directly change package state. The surrounding context is legitimate dependency upgrade guidance, so this is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Remote Content Retrieval Example
medium_findings.description: The skill includes a curl command for reading a public React changelog. The URL is relevant to upgrade research, but remote content should be inspected before use in automation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of a network command to GitHub. It fetches public documentation and does not transmit secrets, so the risk is moderate.

medium_findings.title: File Rewriting Migration Script Example
medium_findings.description: The migration script example reads and writes matching source files while replacing React API usage. This is normal migration guidance, but broad globs can unintentionally rewrite many files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fs.readFileSync and fs.writeFileSync calls are explicit and operate over src/**/*.tsx. The code is illustrative, but copied use could alter project files.

medium_findings.title: Rollback Script Contains Destructive Git Commands
medium_findings.description: The rollback example uses git stash, branch checkout, and branch deletion. These commands support rollback planning, but users should verify branch names and uncommitted work first.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell example clearly changes repository state and can delete a branch. The commands are not hidden and match the documented rollback scenario.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at several lines, but the reviewed lines contain prose, front matter, test names, or package upgrade examples. No cryptographic API usage or insecure hashing implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no crypto functions or algorithm configuration. The scanner appears to match substrings inside ordinary text such as description or describe.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned Latest Package Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples use latest package versions and npx tools. This can introduce unexpected major changes or execute package-provided tooling from registries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly invoke npx and package@latest patterns. These are legitimate upgrade actions but carry supply-chain and reproducibility risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Source File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The migration script applies text replacements to every matching TypeScript React file. A dry run and version control review should precede execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The glob and file write calls are explicit. The behavior is useful for codemods but can create widespread unintended changes.

content.value_statement: Major dependency upgrades can break builds, tests, and runtime behavior. This skill provides a staged process for analysis, migration, validation, and rollback.
actual_capabilities: Explains semantic versioning and common upgrade ranges.
Lists package audit, outdated, and dependency tree commands.
Provides staged upgrade steps for major dependency changes.
Shows compatibility matrix and testing strategy examples.
Includes rollback planning and dependency automation guidance.
limitations: Does not verify the latest package versions automatically.
Does not replace project-specific changelog review.
Examples focus mainly on JavaScript and React ecosystems.
Commands can modify files and should be reviewed before use.

use_cases.title: Plan a Framework Upgrade
use_cases.description: Create an ordered plan for moving a React application across major versions with tests after each step.

use_cases.title: Reduce Dependency Risk
use_cases.description: Audit vulnerable packages, review breaking changes, and define a safer update path before remediation.

use_cases.title: Standardize Update Automation
use_cases.description: Configure dependency update tools with limits, labels, schedules, and manual review for major releases.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my dependency list and identify which packages need patch, minor, or major upgrades. Explain the safest order to update them.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Upgrade Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staged dependency upgrade plan for this project. Include compatibility checks, test commands, rollback steps, and review points.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Breaking Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the current and target major versions for these dependencies. Summarize likely breaking changes and code areas to inspect.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Automation Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Renovate or Dependabot policy for this repository. Separate patch, minor, and major updates with testing and approval rules.

output_examples.input: Upgrade React, React DOM, React Router, and testing libraries.
output: A phased upgrade order that starts with TypeScript and test tooling.
Compatibility notes for router and testing library major versions.
Validation steps for unit, integration, visual, and end-to-end tests.

output_examples.input: Prepare a rollback plan for a risky package update.
output: A branch and baseline test plan before the upgrade begins.
Clear rollback checkpoints after failed tests or build errors.
A reminder to protect uncommitted work before branch cleanup.

output_examples.input: Set up automated dependency updates for a repository.
output: A weekly update policy with limits on open pull requests.
Automatic handling for patch and minor updates.
Manual review requirements for major version changes.

best_practices: Upgrade one major dependency at a time and run tests after each change.
Read changelogs, migration guides, and peer dependency requirements before installing updates.
Keep a rollback plan and review lock file changes before merging.
anti_patterns: Do not upgrade every dependency at once without a compatibility plan.
Do not ignore peer dependency warnings or failed tests after an upgrade.
Do not run file rewriting scripts without version control and a review step.

faq.question: Does this skill update dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands, planning guidance, and review steps that a user can apply manually.

faq.question: Which ecosystems does it cover best?
faq.answer: It is strongest for JavaScript, npm, yarn, React, testing libraries, Renovate, and Dependabot workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with security updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes audit commands and staged remediation guidance for vulnerable dependencies.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains commands that can install packages, rewrite files, fetch remote content, and alter git branches.

faq.question: Does it detect all breaking changes?
faq.answer: No. Users still need to read changelogs and test project-specific behavior.

faq.question: Should commands be run exactly as written?
faq.answer: No. Users should adapt package names, branches, and paths to their repository before execution.
